commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a WhatsApp-local effective ack emoji resolver, routes plain ack and status reactions through it, updates WhatsApp docs, and adds focused Vitest coverage.

PR surface: Source +44, Tests +242, Docs +2. Total +288 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection on current main shows a valid WhatsApp `ackReaction` object without `emoji` resolves to an empty string, and the shared WhatsApp ack gate suppresses empty emoji values; I did not execute tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: This PR has no narrow ClawSweeper repair target; the next action is maintainer review/landing because it is protected-labeled and changes compatibility-visible fallback behavior.

target.title: fix(whatsapp): restore ack emoji identity fallback
target.body:
## Summary

WhatsApp ack reactions use an object-shaped config:

```json5
ackReaction: {
  emoji: "👀",
  direct: true,
  group: "mentions",
}
```

Before this change, the WhatsApp runtime read only `ackReaction.emoji`. That meant a valid config such as `{ direct: true, group: "mentions" }` resolved to an empty emoji and suppressed the reaction entirely, even when the responding agent had an identity emoji configured.

This PR adds a small WhatsApp-local resolver for the effective ack emoji and uses it in both WhatsApp reaction paths:

- plain pre-reply ack reactions
- lifecycle status reactions that reuse the ack reaction slot

The resolver preserves existing behavior for the important disable cases:

- no `channels.whatsapp.ackReaction` config still means no reaction
- explicit `emoji: ""` still disables reactions

When the ack object exists but has no `emoji`, WhatsApp now falls back to the routed agent identity emoji, then to the default `👀`. The resolver uses the existing agent identity SDK helper so normalized agent ids keep working.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/ack-emoji.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/ack-reaction.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/status-reaction.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply.web-auto-reply.last-route.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply.broadcast-groups.combined.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply.web-auto-reply.connection-and-logging.e2e.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply.web-auto-reply.compresses-common-formats-jpeg-cap.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- Signed commit verified locally with `git log -1 --show-signature`

`node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"` was attempted for broad Testbox proof, but Blacksmith Testbox did not return a `tbx_` id (`blacksmith testbox warmup did not print a tbx_ id`).

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: WhatsApp ack/status reactions now use the routed agent identity emoji when `channels.whatsapp.ackReaction` is configured without an `emoji` field.

Real environment tested: Local mocked WhatsApp Vitest coverage in the OpenClaw checkout.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/ack-emoji.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/ack-reaction.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/status-reaction.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply.web-auto-reply.last-route.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply.broadcast-groups.combined.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply.web-auto-reply.connection-and-logging.e2e.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply.web-auto-reply.compresses-common-formats-jpeg-cap.test.ts`

Evidence after fix: New tests cover missing `emoji`, explicit empty emoji disable, configured emoji, default fallback, normalized agent ids, plain ack reactions, status reactions, and the shared WhatsApp auto-reply harness import path.

Observed result after fix: The focused Vitest command exited 0.

What was not tested: Live WhatsApp send. Broad remote `pnpm check:changed` was attempted through Crabbox/Testbox but blocked before a Testbox id was allocated.
target.author: mcaxtr
